title: Breaking: NASA Administrator: Artemis II Is 'Just the Beginning' — Permanent Moon Base and Mars on Horizon
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman declared that Artemis II is only the opening act of a much larger ambition, signaling long-term program momentum toward a permanent lunar base by 2030 and eventual Mars missions.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Apr 10, 2026
summary: Artemis II mission complete: Orion splashes down off San Diego at approximately 8:07 p.m. EDT on April 10, completing humanity's first crewed lunar flyby since Apollo 17. Events were added for Flight Days 7–10 covering the first return correction burn and lunar sphere-of-influence exit on April 7, key engineering tests and crew media Q&A on April 8, final deep-space reentry preparations on April 9, and the splashdown on April 10. The timeline was updated with the lunar flyby distance record (Apr 6) and splashdown (Apr 10) as major milestones. KPIs and meta reflect mission completion status.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Thu Apr 9, 2026
summary: Flight Day 9 was the final full day in deep space for the Artemis II crew.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: On Flight Day 8, the Artemis II crew conducted key engineering tests during the return coast to Earth, providing additional mission data ahead of re-entry.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: Flight Day 7 marked the beginning of the homeward leg of Artemis II.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: Flight Day 6 of Artemis II delivered history: Orion flew within 4,070 miles of the Moon at 7:02 p.m. EDT and the crew broke Apollo 13's 1970 distance record, reaching 252,706 miles from Earth — the farthest any humans have ever traveled. Events were added for Flight Days 3 through 6 covering the trajectory correction burn cancellation, deep-space manual piloting tests by Koch and Hansen, full OCSS suit demonstrations, an outbound trajectory correction burn, and the full lunar flyby with 30 scientific observation targets including the Orientale basin. KPIs were updated to reflect the lunar flyby completion and the new crewed distance record.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: On Flight Day 5, all four Artemis II crew members completed a comprehensive Orion Crew Survival System (OCSS) suit demonstration sequence — a primary test objective of the mission.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: On Flight Day 4, Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen each took turns manually controlling the Orion spacecraft in a 41-minute deep-space piloting evaluation beginning at 9:09 p.m.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: Flight controllers at Johnson Space Center elected to cancel the first planned outbound trajectory correction burn scheduled for Flight Day 3, April 3.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: Artemis II launched successfully on April 1, 2026 at 6:35 p.m. EDT — the first crewed mission beyond low Earth orbit since Apollo 17 in December 1972. Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen are currently en route to the Moon on a 10-day free-return flyby. Two minor countdown holds (FTS ground hardware and an LAS battery sensor anomaly) were resolved before liftoff. On Mission Day 2 (April 2), Glover completed a planned hour-long manual Orion piloting test, and the crew resolved a toilet malfunction before their first sleep period. All systems are nominal.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: NASA's SLS Block 1 rocket carrying the Orion spacecraft (callsign: Integrity) lifted off from Launch Complex 39B at Kennedy Space Center at 6:35:12 p.m. Artemis II Crew Boards Orion — Suit-Up, White Room Signing, Hatch Closure.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: NASA teams at Kennedy Space Center completed final launch readiness activities for Artemis II ahead of the April 1 launch attempt.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: NASA's 45th Weather Squadron issued its official forecast for the April 1, 2026 Artemis II launch attempt, predicting 80% favorable conditions at Kennedy Space Center's LC-39B.

title: Artemis Program Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: Artemis II's SLS/Orion completed rollout to Launch Complex 39B on March 20 and remains on track for a no-earlier-than April 1 launch at 6:24 p.m. EDT. In a major policy shift, NASA Administrator Isaacman hosted the 'Ignition' event on March 24, formally cancelling the Lunar Gateway and announcing a $20 billion, 7-year program to build a permanent lunar surface base targeting sustained human presence after 2032. The Artemis II crew — Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen — entered pre-launch quarantine and are flying to Kennedy Space Center on March 27 to complete final countdown preparations. Claims and political sections have been updated to reflect the Gateway cancellation and Isaacman's revised architecture framing.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: Artemis II Ground Teams Complete Pad Engineering Tests at LC-39B.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: NASA 'Ignition' Event: Lunar Gateway Cancelled, $20B Moon Base Plan Announced.

title: Artemis II Mission Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
